action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent error is due to file truncation in workspace (train.py cuts off mid-debug/loop after printing step 0 debug, so executed code has incomplete tensor prep, missing forward/loss/optimizer/save entirely; predict.py also truncated before completing bbox/point logic + JSON dump). Debug shows consistent 112 tokens/features but error raised anyway due to malformed inputs from truncation. test_prep confirms good data (~7k samples, bbox+point in abs pixels). Analysis: Truncation from long scripts with excessive debugs/comments is root cause after 17 failed iters; must provide much shorter, complete, non-truncated code without per-step debugs (only initial load + periodic loss). Use standard single full-template processor call (no dual calls), standard min/max_pixels=256*28*28, compact LoRA (r=8 on LM modules only), simple manual bs=1 loop (32 samples/8 steps for <30s iter), robust header-based masking, metrics.jsonl, full save. Complete predict.py with all parsers, rescale from 1000-grid using per-image w/h, clipping, center default, full JSON output. This will produce first successful train + baseline grounding_score. Decision: Overwrite train.py + predict.py with compact complete versions, run test_prep+train, submit_eval for first score + time meas before scaling samples/epochs/LR.
